mission.title: Install and Configure Piraeus Datastore on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Piraeus Operator manages LINSTOR clusters in Kubernetes, providing a robust solution for software-defined storage. Installing it allows you to easily deploy and manage replicated block storage in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Install Linux kernel headers
steps.description:
Piraeus uses DRBD for storage replication, which requires Linux kernel headers on all nodes. Install them on each node:
```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu
apt-get install -y linux-headers-$(uname -r)
# RHEL/CentOS
yum install -y kernel-devel-$(uname -r)
```

steps.title: Deploy the Piraeus Operator
steps.description:
Apply the Piraeus Operator manifest. This creates the `piraeus-datastore` namespace and deploys the operator:
```bash
kubectl apply --server-side -f https://github.com/piraeusdatastore/piraeus-operator/releases/latest/download/manifest.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the operator is running
steps.description:
Check that the Piraeus Operator pod is running in the piraeus-datastore namespace:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n piraeus-datastore
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/piraeus-operator-controller-manager -n piraeus-datastore
```

steps.title: Create a LinstorCluster resource
steps.description:
Deploy a LinstorCluster custom resource to set up the actual storage cluster:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: piraeus.io/v1
kind: LinstorCluster
metadata:
  name: linstorcluster
spec: {}
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify storage is ready
steps.description:
Wait for LINSTOR satellite pods to start on each node and verify the storage classes:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n piraeus-datastore
kubectl get sc | grep piraeus
```

uninstall.title: Remove the LinstorCluster resource
uninstall.description:
First, delete the LinstorCluster custom resource:
```bash
kubectl delete linstorclusters.piraeus.io --all
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Piraeus Operator
uninstall.description:
Delete the operator manifest and clean up the namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/piraeusdatastore/piraeus-operator/releases/latest/download/manifest.yaml
kubectl delete namespace piraeus-datastore
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Piraeus Operator
upgrade.description:
Re-apply the latest manifest to upgrade the operator in-place:
```bash
kubectl apply --server-side -f https://github.com/piraeusdatastore/piraeus-operator/releases/latest/download/manifest.yaml
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/piraeus-operator-controller-manager -n piraeus-datastore
```

troubleshooting.title: Satellite pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If satellite pods fail to start, verify kernel headers are installed on the affected nodes:
```bash
kubectl logs -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/component=linstor-satellite --tail=50
```
Missing kernel headers prevent DRBD module compilation.

troubleshooting.title: Storage class not created
troubleshooting.description:
If no StorageClass appears after install, check the LinstorCluster status:
```bash
kubectl describe linstorclusters.piraeus.io linstorcluster
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Piraeus will have the operator and satellite pods running in the piraeus-datastore namespace, with LINSTOR storage classes available for PVC provisioning.

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster with Linux kernel headers installed on all nodes (for DRBD storage replication).
